title: Vicia saxatilis
taxonomy: Vicia saxatilis is classified under the kingdom Plantae, with its more specific classification being: Vicia saxatilis, in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume family. This classification places it within the order Fabales, which is a group of flowering plants that are primarily known for their pea-like flowers and seed pods. The family Fabaceae is further divided into several subfamilies, including the subfamily Faboideae, to which Vicia saxatilis belongs. Vicia saxatilis is a species within the genus Vicia, which includes a variety of vetches and other legumes. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Vicia saxatilis, also known as Rock Vetch, is a perennial legume species that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to ease congestion and soothe the throat. The bioactive constituents of Rock Vetch include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its health benefits. Rock Vetch is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine as a cough syrup and expectorant to help relieve respiratory symptoms.
